prompt: A traditional Japanese Ukiyo-e woodblock print. A central toll-gate official stands on a steep, dramatic diagonal mountain path. He is actively directing three distinct, differently colored groups of travelers down three separate, winding trails that diverge into the distance. Flat, unmodulated colors using a strict palette of Prussian blue, muted ochre, and vermilion. Crisp, stark black ink outlines define every figure and path. The texture of pressed mulberry paper and faint woodgrain from the carved printing blocks is visible. No text, no modern clothing, no 3D shading or gradients.
tradition_approach: Applied strong diagonal compositions, crisp black key-block outlines, visible woodgrain texture, and flat areas of unmodulated Prussian blue to mimic the physical and compositional constraints of woodblock printing.
